# kian.coffee — expanded summary

> Kian Alikhani — full-stack IC architect at Mastercard (Arlington, VA).
> This is the RAG-friendly long-form companion to /llms.txt. Everything
> here is sourced from the CV at projects/kian-coffee/docs/cv.md or from
> public repository state.

## Professional summary

Full-stack IC architect at a 600+ engineer organization, building
end-to-end across frontend, backend, data, and platform at Mastercard.
Swiss-army-knife generalist who ships real systems: a real-time,
event-driven data pipeline at millions of transactions per hour on
Kafka, Spark, and Hadoop ($6M first-year revenue, concept-to-prod in
3 months); an enterprise design system and UI library adopted across
7 global programs and 20+ teams (25% engineering velocity,
50% DX satisfaction); and, most recently, AI-first foundations
(MCP servers, reusable skills, knowledge-base standardization) as a
core member of a CTO-led task force transforming SDLC/PDLC.
Self-hosted hybrid-cloud home lab on K3s / Talos and Proxmox exercises
the same generalist instincts on private infrastructure — networking,
storage, IaC, and a local LLM stack as one layer among many.

## Work experience

### Mastercard — Lead Software Engineer, 2023 – present

- Architected AI-first foundations for the engineering org: reusable
  skills, MCP servers, agent patterns, and a knowledge-base pipeline
  that takes raw intake through standardization to a consistent display
  layer. Leads knowledge-transfer sessions rolling these foundations
  across teams.
- Designed and delivered a customer-facing AI-empowered product
  end-to-end — from design and product framing to executive pitch to
  integration. Modernized legacy workstreams so they plug into new
  AI-native workstreams as they come online.
- Core member of a CTO-led task force transforming the SDLC and PDLC
  with second-brain capabilities across engineers, product managers,
  and designers — breaking down silos across 3 service groups and 20+
  teams.
- Led the enterprise AI platform POC that established the scalable
  foundation for compound AI systems across the org.
- Led planning, design, and rollout of a new design system and UI
  library adopted across 7 global programs and 20+ engineering teams;
  improved engineering velocity 25% and developer satisfaction 50%
  over the previous implementation.

### Mastercard — Senior Software Engineer, 2020 – 2023

- Designed and delivered a highly available data pipeline processing
  millions of transactions per hour on Kafka, Spark, and Hadoop; took
  it from concept to production in 3 months and generated $6M in
  first-year revenue with continued YoY growth.
- Designed and implemented role-based access control (RBAC) for an
  internal portal, unifying access to 15 microservices across 6+ user
  roles.
- Led the modernization to a micro-frontend architecture, reducing
  load times, dependency collision, and upgrade complexity across the
  portfolio.
- Built scalable retry and circuit-breaker patterns with dynamic
  queuing and autoscaling to handle burst traffic gracefully.

### Conseqta Technology — Software Engineer, 2019 – 2020

- Led modernization efforts at an 8-person startup: migrated AngularJS
  to Angular and monolithic REST services to Spring Boot microservices.
- Owned feature delivery across the full stack — infrastructure,
  backend, and UI.
- Established a GitOps deployment pipeline using GitLab Workflows for
  stateless microservices across on-prem and cloud Kubernetes
  environments.

### IBM Watson — Software Engineer Intern, Summer 2018

(Per CV: role only. Details pending.)

## Self-hosted projects (from the CV)

- **AI-first monitoring + IaC** — Local LLM drives Ansible-based setup
  of Prometheus + Grafana stacks, evaluates and reinforces monitoring
  coverage, triages incoming alerts, and auto-tunes thresholds and
  alert/scrape parameters based on observed signal. Agentic ops on a
  self-hosted stack.
- **Self-hosted hybrid cloud** — Highly available multi-AZ Kubernetes
  cluster on K3s + Proxmox (currently migrating to Talos), declarative
  rollouts via Ansible, ArgoCD, and GitHub Actions.
- **AI-enabled home automation** — Home Assistant + N8N + local LLM
  orchestration for private, low-latency automation; keeps inference
  and data on-prem.
- **Zero-trust home network** — Deny-by-default, allowlist-based access
  model with VPN, VLAN segmentation, and DMZ firewalls.
- **Multi-AZ NAS with 3-2-1 backups** — TrueNAS + ZFS with Cloud Sync
  and RSync for data durability and archival across availability zones.

## Education

- B.S. Computer Science, Case Western Reserve University (2018)
- Thomas Jefferson High School for Science and Technology (2015)

## Skills (from the CV)

- **Languages:** Java, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, SQL
- **Frontend:** Angular, React, Node.js, micro frontends
- **Backend / Data:** Spring Boot, Kafka, Spark, Hadoop, RabbitMQ,
  event-driven architecture, circuit breakers, retry/backoff,
  autoscaling, PostgreSQL, OracleSQL, MongoDB
- **Infra / DevOps:** Kubernetes (K3s / Talos), Docker, Proxmox,
  Ansible, ArgoCD, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitOps, CI/CD, IaC,
  multi-AZ HA, PCF, networking (VLANs, VPN, firewalls)
- **AI / Platform:** MCP servers, agent patterns / orchestration,
  reusable skills, RAG (Chroma DB), vector search, self-hosted /
  local LLMs, knowledge-base standardization, org-scale AI foundations
  (skills repository with deduplication + auto-update), LLM-driven
  automation (Ansible, monitoring, alerting)
- **Architecture:** distributed systems, high-throughput data
  platforms, resilience patterns, design-system rollouts, enterprise
  SDLC/PDLC transformation
